title: Agent Ergonomics And Token Evaluation Loop
goal: Run adaptive model CLI evaluation loops focused on agent ergonomics, token usage, and obvious next-step routing, then patch product/harness/docs surfaces where repeated ambiguity or avoidable context load appears.
non_goals: Do not turn the harness into a formal deterministic benchmark.
Do not optimize only for passing metadata checks; maintainer judgment over dynamic behavior is primary evidence.
Do not broaden into mixed-agent workflow testing in this slice.

intent.outcome: Make the correct next action more visible and compact for agents, and formalize asking agents for post-mortem feedback after evaluation runs.
intent.constraints: Keep prompts realistic; avoid explicitly steering agents to use Agentic Workspace unless the scenario is about config/proof discovery. Prefer compact CLI outputs and narrow read surfaces.
intent.latitude: Add/refine harness scenarios, post-mortem prompts, compact report/summary output, docs, tests, and bounded CLI projections when dynamic runs expose repeated ambiguity.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: Run targeted dynamic evaluations, ask agents post-run why they chose their workflow, patch repeated ergonomic/token issues, and rerun the smallest useful scenario set.
execution.proof: Harness tests, targeted workspace CLI tests, selected real model runs, and make check before closeout.

invariants: Dynamic evaluator judgment outranks formal score totals.
Agents should see one obvious next step before high-volume detail.
Post-mortem self-feedback should be compact, optional evidence that helps improve the package, not another required human-facing ritual.
Compact outputs must stay sufficient for action and route to deeper detail only when needed.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make Agentic Workspace cheaper and clearer for agents to use correctly across model CLIs.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Reusable postmortem feedback in model CLI evaluations, smaller idle summary output, compact config first-contact routing, and explicit warnings for avoidable raw config reads.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Copilot and Codex still spend large provider/runtime context budgets even when package prompts are compact; stale fixture guidance can make agents hallucinate removed commands.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: make check passed after focused tests and real Copilot, Codex, and Gemini harness runs.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue adaptive optimisation when new model runs expose recurring ambiguity

intent_interpretation.literal request: Run more evaluation loops and optimise for agent ergonomics and token usage; formalise asking agents themselves for feedback during post-mortem.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Use real model behavior and agent self-explanation to find where package outputs make correct workflow expensive or ambiguous, then tighten compact surfaces.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Add post-mortem feedback support to the harness workflow, run selected model probes, reduce high-volume startup/summary surfaces where possible, and validate with focused tests plus make check.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check whether changes make the next action more visible without hiding necessary proof or routing context.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: scripts/model_cli_harness/**; tools/model-cli-harness/**; docs/maintainer/model-cli-dogfooding-harness.md; src/agentic_workspace/cli.py; tests/test_model_cli_harness.py; tests/test_workspace_cli.py; active execplan/state closeout
execution_bounds.max changed files: Around 12 files unless compact CLI changes require generated references/contracts.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_model_cli_harness.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; selected real harness runs; make check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, model CLI harness script/suite/docs, selected run outputs, compact start/summary/report/config output, and narrow CLI tests.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Run dynamic probes, ask post-run feedback, patch repeated ambiguity/token load, rerun focused probes, prove with tests and make check.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Agent ergonomics and token-use optimisation loop.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not reduce safety by hiding proof/state obligations; post-mortem feedback should inform maintainer judgment, not become a score.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

immediate_next_action: Run targeted dynamic evaluations and inspect post-run ergonomics/token-use friction before patching.
invariants: Correct next steps should be visible before deep detail.
Compact outputs should avoid unnecessary token load but preserve enough context to act safely.
Post-mortem agent feedback should ask why choices were made and what would have prevented mistakes.
validation_commands: selected real harness runs for Copilot, Codex, and Gemini where provider availability allows
completion_criteria: Dynamic model evidence is collected for at least two target adapters, with Gemini attempted unless provider/runtime blocks it.
Post-mortem feedback prompting is documented and supported by the harness workflow.
Repeated next-step or token-load friction found in the runs is fixed or routed.
Focused tests and make check pass.
The active plan is closed or explicitly routes remaining residue.

execution_run.scope touched: model CLI harness script/suite/docs/fixtures, workspace CLI startup/config strings, planning compact summary projection, tests, generated llms surface, active planning state

execution_run.result for continuation: No required continuation; future optimisation loops can use --postmortem-feedback and the new raw-read warning.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The requested loop ran across all three configured adapters where provider availability allowed, immediate findings were fixed, and validation passed.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: harness run roots under scratch/model-cli-harness; make check log; updated harness docs/tests
closure_check.reopen trigger: a future model evaluation repeats avoidable raw reads, stale command routing, or excessive compact-output token load

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: Copilot postmortem prompt initially included raw transcript tail and doubled token load.
Codex read raw config files even after compact config was the intended source.
Gemini repeated stale fixture guidance for removed agentic-workspace planning command.
Idle planning summary still carried repeated unavailable projection detail.
signals fixed: Postmortem prompt now uses extracted final answer and a tighter cap.
Harness metadata can warn on forbidden executed commands with Windows path normalization.
Configured fixture says not to read raw config when compact output is sufficient.
Minimal/invalid planning fixtures no longer mention removed planning command or durable_state_bridge.
Idle compact summary omits repeated unavailable detail and clean-only authoring affordances while preserving meaningful historical evidence.

drift_log: 2026-05-04: Ran Copilot, Codex, and Gemini optimisation probes; patched compactness, postmortem, raw-read warnings, and stale fixture routing; make check passed.
